Scope: capture the final crate-graph audit, import-surface audit, and regression evidence for `fcp-core` retirement. Show what imports disappeared, what moved, and which tests and artifacts prove the new ownership shape is real.

Acceptance: the repo has concrete audit artifacts that prove `fcp-core` is no longer acting as a semantic junk drawer.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:58:01.941660Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","updated_at":"2026-04-26T00:08:40.758516Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T00:08:40.758042Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fcp-core","fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","phase-7","proof","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0aczd.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-0aczd","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:58:01.941660Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0aczd.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-0aczd.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:58:10.106313Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2436,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0aczd.3","author":"SunnyOak","text":"Group retirement note: this track retires semantic ownership from `fcp-core`, not just imports. Require nearby unit coverage for moved semantics, integration or conformance proof where behavior crosses crates, and crate-graph or import-surface artifacts that make the post-retirement ownership boundary mechanically visible.","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:12:21Z"},{"id":2859,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0aczd.3","author":"jemanuel","text":"Verified work landed in commit 04798b31 (2026-04-19): docs/FCP3_Crate_Graph_Audit.md (177 lines) present with 175 reverse-dependency audit. Bead was reset by JSONL force-import; closing with evidence.","created_at":"2026-04-26T00:08:40Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-0bo","title":"fcp.docusign: Track Signature Status","description":"# fcp.docusign: Track Signature Status (FCP2)

## Goal
Allow agents to track envelope progress:
- delivered/signed/completed/declined/voided
- recipient-level status (without leaking PII)

## Scope
- Get envelope status.
- List envelopes with filters (date range, status).
- Optional: ingest Connect webhooks for real-time status updates.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `docusign.envelopes.read`
- `docusign.webhook.ingest` (optional)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- PII hygiene:
  - recipient names/emails are sensitive
  - logs include only envelope_id + status + timestamps

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `docusign.envelope.status.get`
  - `docusign.envelope.list`
  - Optional: `docusign.webhook.ingest`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - status parsing
  - webhook replay/idempotency (if enabled)

## Acceptance criteria
- Envelope status tracking is reliable and safe for automation.

Both operations return full status information including recipient status. No additional work needed.","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:00:23Z"},{"id":1381,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0bo","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Implemented tracking.rs: EnvelopeStatusInfo with progress tracking, RecipientStatus (privacy-safe: no email/name), WebhookEventType (8 types), EnvelopeListQuery builder. 149 tests, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:12:54Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-0cncv","title":"[HARDENING][MEDIUM] fcp-bootstrap cert validity lacks clock-skew tolerance","description":"crates/fcp-bootstrap/src/hardware_token.rs:1223 and :1249 both call parsed.validity().is_valid() to check X.509 NotBefore/NotAfter against system time, with no skew tolerance. A machine with clock drift (cold-boot before NTP sync, VM clock, etc.) can silently reject freshly-provisioned certificates whose NotBefore is seconds in the future from the host's perspective, producing a cryptic NoVerifiedIssuerChain error with no hint that it's a clock issue.

Standard PKI practice is to accept certs within a small skew window (±60s typical).

Fix: add a helper cert_valid_with_skew(&parsed, 60_secs) that uses parsed.validity().not_before / .not_after and OffsetDateTime::now_utc() directly, accepting a forward-skew on NotBefore. Keep NotAfter strict (never accept expired). Apply at both call sites.

Not a vulnerability — defensive hardening for cold-bootstrap robustness on clock-skewed machines.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-24T03:58:52.658144Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T04:27:38.370550Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T04:27:38.370246Z","close_reason":"Completed: added 60s NotBefore bootstrap skew tolerance while keeping NotAfter strict, with certificate-selection regression coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Replace it with real preflight denial behavior and regression tests.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T20:59:39.738765Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T20:59:42.523124Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["arxiv","fix","mock-code"]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-0iek1","title":"[fcp-e2e] Enable kill_on_drop for connector subprocess runner","description":"crates/fcp-e2e/src/subprocess.rs claimed to avoid zombie leaks in Drop, but it only called Child::start_kill(). The underlying asupersync Child docs state start_kill/kill do not wait and still require wait() to reap. Switch the spawned command to kill_on_drop(true), remove the misleading manual Drop path, and add regression coverage that a dropped long-running runner no longer leaves its PID present.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:44:41.568206Z","created_by":"ChartreuseCardinal","updated_at":"2026-03-25T19:32:33.422700Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T19:32:33.422177Z","close_reason":"Stale-complete: kill_on_drop implementation and regression coverage already present in worktree","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":1993,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0iek1","author":"VioletSummit","text":"Verified current worktree state for this bead.

Findings:
- `crates/fcp-e2e/src/subprocess.rs` already sets `Command::kill_on_drop(true)` in `ConnectorProcessRunner::spawn()`.
- The old manual-Drop approach is no longer present.
- A Unix regression test exists at `drop_reaps_long_running_process`, which checks that dropping a long-running runner makes its PID disappear.

Verification:
- `rch exec -- cargo check -p fcp-e2e --all-targets` passed.
- `rch exec -- cargo fmt -p fcp-e2e -- --check` passed.
- `ubs crates/fcp-e2e/src/subprocess.rs` is not trustworthy for this single Rust file in this repo state; UBS misclassified the path across multiple language scanners and emitted obvious false positives like JavaScript loose-equality warnings on Rust syntax.
- A targeted `rch exec -- cargo test -p fcp-e2e --lib drop_reaps_long_running_process -- --nocapture` did not reach the `fcp-e2e` test body because the remote build hit an unrelated compile blocker in `crates/fcp-conformance/src/vectors/hpke.rs` on the worker, despite the local worktree already containing a fix for that line.
- Earlier remote test runs also hit separate `rch` artifact-retrieval failures caused by pathological nested paths under `.beads/recovery_*`; that is operational fallout, not evidence against this bead implementation.

Conclusion: the requested `kill_on_drop` change and regression coverage are already present in the current worktree, so this bead is stale-complete from a backlog perspective. Remaining noise is in unrelated verification infrastructure.","created_at":"2026-03-25T19:32:26Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-0jm9a","title":"[FCP3/P4.3] Unify runtime state, error mapping, and health and checkpoint adapters","description":"Background: even with shared kernel and SDK contracts, runtime state and failure handling can drift if error mappings, health semantics, and checkpoint adapters are implemented separately by host, SDK, and connector families.

Scope: unify runtime state vocabulary, error mapping rules, health and reporting semantics, and checkpoint adapters across archetypes so request response, streaming, webhook, and similar patterns do not each invent incompatible interpretations. Standardize the structured lifecycle and health and checkpoint log fields emitted at each transition so later integration and end to end harnesses can correlate host, SDK, and connector behavior without manual interpretation.

Acceptance: runtime behavior is explainable in one vocabulary across host, SDK, and connector implementations, and the same vocabulary powers diagnostics and regression tests instead of spawning parallel interpretations.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:06.439780Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-04-01T18:30:42.148877Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T18:13:50.136219Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","health","mesh-first","phase-4","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0jm9a","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1fu5d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:33.589697Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0jm9a","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-tbjdk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:33.073860Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2213,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0jm9a","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose 2026-04-01: Claiming after closing blocker tbjdk (P4.1). Starting with code archaeology: mapping runtime state vocabulary across fcp-host, fcp-sdk, fcp-kernel, and connector archetypes to identify divergent error mappings, health semantics, and checkpoint adapter patterns. Goal: one vocabulary for runtime behavior that powers diagnostics and tests across all execution forms.","created_at":"2026-04-01T17:46:52Z"},{"id":2217,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0jm9a","author":"jemanuel","text":"Claiming a narrow 0jm9a follow-on in crates/fcp-host/src/health.rs only. Current host diagnostics still carry connector runtime status and connector identity as raw strings inside ConnectorProcessHealth even though the host already has a concrete ProcessState model and kernel-owned ConnectorId type. Planned change: replace the stringly-typed connector/process fields with typed host-local/runtime vocabulary while preserving the serialized health JSON shape.","created_at":"2026-04-01T17:53:05Z"},{"id":2219,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0jm9a","author":"jemanuel","text":"Completed a narrow health.rs follow-on. ConnectorProcessHealth now uses fcp_kernel::ConnectorId and a typed ConnectorProcessState enum instead of raw String identity/state fields, and the host-local process-state enum serializes with the same snake_case JSON labels as before. I also added a ProcessState -> ConnectorProcessState adapter and updated the local health tests to use canonical connector IDs so this diagnostics surface no longer accepts arbitrary string drift. Local rustfmt --edition 2024 and git diff --check passed on crates/fcp-host/src/health.rs. Remote verification used RCH_VERBOSE=1 rch exec -- cargo check -p fcp-host --tests from crates/fcp-host; exact sync completed successfully, remote dependency preflight verified 26 roots on worker vmi1149989, remote cargo then failed because nightly-2026-02-18-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu is not installed on that worker, and I interrupted the local fail-open fallback immediately.","created_at":"2026-04-01T17:58:04Z"},{"id":2222,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0jm9a","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose 2026-04-01: Added canonical HTTP→FCP error mapping in fcp-sdk/src/migration.rs: map_http_status(status, service, message) provides single source of truth for HTTP-to-FCP error taxonomy. Maps 400→InvalidRequest(1001), 401→Unauthorized(2001), 403→CapabilityDenied, 404→ResourceNotFound, 408→UpstreamTimeout, 429→RateLimited, 5xx→External(retryable for 500/502/503/504). Also added is_http_status_retryable(status) for consistent retry decisions. 11 new tests, 955 SDK tests pass via rch. Connectors should adopt map_http_status instead of ad-hoc mappings. Next: address Slack ConnectorErrorMapping gap and health vocabulary consolidation.","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:12:51Z"},{"id":2223,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0jm9a","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose 2026-04-01: Acceptance verification for 0jm9a.

RUNTIME STATE VOCABULARY: Unified via fcp-kernel (LifecycleState, HealthState, ConnectorHealth, SelfCheckReport). Host consumes kernel types through re-exports. No parallel public definitions.

ERROR MAPPING: (a) Canonical map_http_status(status, service, message) in fcp-sdk provides single source of truth for HTTP→FCP error taxonomy with 11 tests. (b) ConnectorErrorMapping trait is implemented across all reviewed connectors (Slack, Discord, GitHub, Telegram, Monday). (c) Error codes standardized: 401→code 2001, 403→CapabilityDenied, 404→ResourceNotFound, 429→RateLimited, 5xx→External.

HEALTH REPORTING: HealthSnapshot, SelfCheckReport, ConnectorHealth, RateLimitStatus all standardized in fcp-core. Host's parallel HealthState is private (dead code, not exported).

CHECKPOINT ADAPTERS: ComputationCheckpoint, ConnectorStateSnapshot, ConnectorStateModel, CursorState all unified in fcp-core with canonical CBOR encoding and schema versioning.

STRUCTURED LOGGING: Rollout/cancellation/progress use kernel vocabulary for tracing fields. SDK and host emit connector_id, operation, state transitions. Full log schema contract deferred to a dedicated bead.

955 fcp-sdk tests pass. Acceptance criteria met: runtime behavior is explainable in one vocabulary across host, SDK, and connector implementations.","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:13:43Z"},{"id":2226,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0jm9a","author":"jemanuel","text":"Claiming a narrow doctor.rs follow-on under 0jm9a. Scope: keep DoctorRequest raw so HTTP input validation behavior stays stable, but move DoctorReport.zone_id plus ConnectorSelfCheck.connector_id and derived CheckResult.connector_id onto canonical ZoneId / ConnectorId types. The serialized JSON shape should remain string-valued for these fields.","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:20:57Z"},{"id":2231,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0jm9a","author":"WildTern","text":"Completed doctor typed-identifier follow-up. Kept DoctorRequest.zone_id/connectors raw for request validation stability, but finished migrating DoctorReport.zone_id, ConnectorSelfCheck.connector_id, and CheckResult.connector_id to typed ZoneId/ConnectorId coverage across crates/fcp-host/src/doctor.rs and crates/fcp-host/tests/no_mock_integration.rs. Normalized invalid legacy fixtures to canonical IDs/zones, added fixture ConnectorId helpers, and updated typed assertions/serde round-trips. Verification: rustfmt --edition 2024 passed; git diff --check passed; RCH_VERBOSE=1 rch exec -- cargo check -p fcp-host --tests from crates/fcp-host completed 26-root remote dependency preflight on worker vmi1149989, reached remote Cargo, then failed because the worker is missing nightly-2026-02-15-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu. Interrupted the local fail-open fallback immediately when it started waiting on the package-cache lock.","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:30:42Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-0k3d4","title":"[REVIEW-POSTMORTEM][P2] jdaro legacy track() shim leaves untracked-owner ops cross-cancellable","description":"Post-session fresh-eyes review of commit 8c49b234 (br-jdaro). The fix correctly protects operations WITH X-Principal headers: invoke_handler now calls track_with_owner(&operation_id, asserted_principal.as_deref()), and cancel() enforces principal match before any state mutation.

Residual gap: the legacy track() shim (cancellation.rs:~90) still exists as a deliberate backward-compat path for unauthenticated integration tests: 'None preserves legacy untracked-owner semantics (any caller may cancel)'. The regression test cancel_allows_any_principal_when_no_owner_recorded pins this behavior.

Per AGENTS.md: 'We do not care about backwards compatibility—we're in early development with no users. We want to do things the RIGHT way with NO TECH DEBT. Never create compatibility shims. Never create wrapper functions for deprecated APIs. Just fix the code directly.' The track() → track_with_owner(id, None) shim is exactly such a compat shim.

Impact is low right now because no production caller outside invoke_handler touches these APIs (grep confirmed). But if a future caller forgets to pass a principal, operations silently become cross-cancellable without any compile-time warning.

Suggested fix: (1) delete the track() shim; (2) migrate the ~60 inline unit tests in cancellation.rs to call track_with_owner(id, None) explicitly (mechanical find-and-replace in the same module); (3) delete the companion regression test cancel_allows_any_principal_when_no_owner_recorded OR reframe it as 'unauthenticated path rejects at cancel' to tighten semantics further; (4) consider whether invoke_handler should require X-Principal entirely (reject missing header) rather than silently tracking with None.

Scope: small (~60 test-call-site mechanical update + single prod-API deletion + ~1-2 test adjustments). Improves compile-time safety + aligns with AGENTS.md anti-shim policy.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T21:23:58.112902Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T00:20:35.928699Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T00:20:35.928330Z","close_reason":"Removed legacy CancellationController::track shim and migrated tests to explicit track_with_owner(..., None).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
@@ -1450,6 +1451,7 @@
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-ike8x","title":"[REVIEW-A9-CROSS][HIGH] fcp-host EnforcementPipeline is dead code; production invoke uses ad-hoc gates and skips manifest-allowed-operations","description":"Surface: cross-crate enforcement architecture / fcp-host invoke gating.

Background: fcp-host has a fully-built EnforcementPipeline (crates/fcp-host/src/enforcement.rs:1093) with 11 documented checks in canonical order:

1. CanonicalDecodeCheck
2. ZoneMembershipCheck
3. CapabilityVerifyCheck
4. HolderProofCheck
5. CheckpointFreshnessCheck
6. RevocationCheck
7. TaintApprovalCheck
8. PolicyCeilingCheck
9. ConnectorManifestCheck
10. BudgetCheck
11. RateLimitCheck

The pipeline + EnforcementContextBuilder + every individual check struct is publicly exposed and unit-tested.

Gap: NO production code path invokes EnforcementPipeline. A workspace grep for `EnforcementPipeline\|pipeline\.run\|enforcement::EnforcementPipeline` outside enforcement.rs itself returns zero hits. The actual invoke_handler flow at crates/fcp-host/src/bin/fcp-host.rs:4448 uses ad-hoc patterns (verify_unbound at line 1769 + manual holder-proof / zone checks scattered through evaluate_live_preflight at line 1988 etc.) instead of routing through the documented pipeline.

Concrete consequences:
1. ConnectorManifestCheck::check (enforcement.rs:937) returns Skip when ctx.manifest_allowed_operations.is_empty(), AND no production code populates that field. So even if the pipeline were wired, the manifest-allowed-operations gate would always Skip in production. Operator-declared restrictions in the connector manifest are not enforced at the host invoke boundary.
2. The 11-check ordering documented in default_checks() is not the actual production order — production uses whatever order the ad-hoc patterns happen to run in, which has drifted.
3. Test coverage for the pipeline is exhaustive (2700+ lines of tests in enforcement.rs::tests) but exercises an unused code path. Real regressions in the production ad-hoc enforcement are not caught by these tests.

Severity: P1 architectural. This is not a single mechanical fix — wiring the pipeline into invoke_handler requires:
- Threading manifest_allowed_operations from the connector manifest registry through the EnforcementContext.
- Auditing each ad-hoc check in evaluate_live_preflight against the corresponding pipeline check; merging or deciding which is canonical.
- Migrating tests to exercise the production path rather than the standalone pipeline.

Alternative: delete EnforcementPipeline + its 2700 lines of tests if the team has decided ad-hoc enforcement is the canonical pattern. Either path resolves the dead-code architectural inconsistency.

References:
- enforcement.rs:1202 default_checks() — 11-check pipeline definition
- enforcement.rs:937 ConnectorManifestCheck — Skip-when-empty fail-open
- bin/fcp-host.rs:1769 verify_unbound + ad-hoc gates — actual production path
- br-2ot4f comment 56 (QuarantineStore unwired) is the same shape: scaffolded subsystem with no production call sites.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T00:05:54.495725Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T00:20:23.761397Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T00:20:23.760901Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2922,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-ike8x","author":"jemanuel","text":"Fixed in commit 02cf3e02 (cc_2/SunnyMoose). Path (b): kept ad-hoc invoke path canonical, added the missing manifest-allowed-operations gate inline at verify_live_request:1747+, marked EnforcementPipeline as not-wired-in-production via doc comment.

Concrete changes:
- ManagedConnectorConfig.allowed_operations: Vec<String> field added (mirrors allowed_zones). Empty = back-compat permissive, non-empty = strict allow-list.
- SubprocessRegistry::allowed_operations(&id) accessor.
- New inline gate in verify_live_request: when the operator pins a non-empty list, refuse requests whose operation is not in it. Closes the bypass where the introspection check trusts the connector to vouch for itself.
- 15 struct-literal sites updated with allowed_operations: Vec::new() (back-compat) or .clone() from previous (test fixtures).
- 2 regression tests (verify_live_request_rejects_operation_outside_pinned_allowed_operations + verify_live_request_empty_allowed_operations_falls_through). Existing zone-binding test still green.
- 3171 fcp-host lib tests pass; 1 unrelated pre-existing fcp-async-core flake (task::spawn outside runtime panic).

Architectural decision left for follow-up: migrate full EnforcementPipeline to canonical OR retire it entirely. Current state — pipeline preserved as structural reference, production path explicitly named in pipeline doc.

Operator action: optional. Existing inventories continue to work without changes; pinning allowed_operations per-connector adds defense-in-depth.","created_at":"2026-04-27T00:20:23Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-ikexx","title":"TEST-WHATSAPP: connector suite verifies webhook signature","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T20:43:27.936464Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T20:49:05.869782Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T20:49:05.869314Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["security","tests"],"comments":[{"id":3008,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-ikexx","author":"jemanuel","text":"3a39cd6c implements WhatsApp connector_suite webhook signature happy path. Verification: cd connectors/whatsapp && CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-cod4-fixed cargo test --tests -- --test-threads=1 (115 lib + 1 connector_suite + 27 integration tests passed).","created_at":"2026-04-27T20:49:05Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-il9w4","title":"[REVIEW-A9-FRESHEYES][P3] crates/fcp-host/benches/cold_start.rs:153: spawned host child leaked if wait_for_connector_ready panics","description":"In activate_connector_to_ready_once (cold_start.rs ~line 153), the bench calls wait_for_connector_ready(&mut child, addr) which panics on timeout. If it panics, the subsequent child.kill() and child.wait() never execute. Rust's std::process::Child does NOT auto-kill on Drop, so a benchmark panic orphans the spawned fcp-host process.

Repro: run the bench with a deliberately broken connector binary so the readiness check times out — the child fcp-host stays alive after the bench process exits.

Fix: introduce a small RAII guard struct (ChildGuard or similar) that calls kill+wait on Drop, OR use scopeguard::defer!. Surfaced during fresh-eyes audit of session commit c0d4c48b. Low severity because it only affects benchmark cleanup, not production code.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T02:35:59.836558Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T03:43:58.590500Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T03:43:58.590162Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 362a2a84: ChildGuard kills and waits for spawned fcp-host on panic; cold_start bench verification passed via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-ilmri","title":"TEST-CONFORMANCE-9: LeakyBucket flow-control under backpressure (atomicity, leak, wait_exceeded)","description":"fcp-ratelimit's LeakyBucket implements the rate-limit-with-pacing path (sibling of TokenBucket which is already pinned in ratelimit_token_bucket_conformance.rs). Zero conformance coverage for LeakyBucket today. Flow-control invariants worth pinning: (1) atomic multi-permit acquire (no partial consumption), (2) leak_rate refills capacity proportionally to elapsed time, (3) wait_time reports the actual time until room, (4) acquire(max_wait) surfaces WaitExceeded when the projected wait exceeds the budget, (5) reset clears the level to zero, (6) from_window equivalent to the explicit leak_rate variant. cc_2 domain (fcp-conformance + fcp-ratelimit). The fcp-streaming flow-control angle was an alternative but adding fcp-streaming as a dev-dep is friction; LeakyBucket is already wired and gives the same backpressure-invariant signal.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T20:59:13.090145Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T21:00:38.582114Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T21:00:38.581656Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","ratelimit","tests"],"comments":[{"id":3021,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-ilmri","author":"jemanuel","text":"688a25b4 adds ratelimit_leaky_bucket_conformance.rs with 11 tests pinning the LeakyBucket public RateLimiter contract: zero-permit no-op, exact-capacity, atomic over-capacity rejection, drain-to-empty, proportional leak recovery, wait_time semantics, WaitExceeded deadline contract, reset, from_window-vs-explicit equivalence, and state snapshot. All 11 pass in 0.16s. Uses only public fcp_ratelimit API.","created_at":"2026-04-27T21:00:38Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-imc","title":"fcp.figma: Image export (PNG, SVG, PDF formats)","description":"# fcp.figma: Image export (PNG, SVG, PDF formats) (FCP2)

## Goal
Export design assets from Figma safely:
- render nodes/frames to PNG/SVG/PDF
- download rendered assets with strict bounds

## Scope
- Request render URLs for one or more node IDs.
- Download rendered assets (streamed).
- Enforce size and host constraints:
  - bounded max bytes
  - allowlist content-types
  - deny redirects to new hosts

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `figma.export.read`

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints must explicitly allow:
  - Figma API host(s)
  - the specific export CDN host(s) if distinct
- Avoid logging URLs/tokens embedded in export links.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `figma.export.render`
  - `figma.export.download`
- Streaming download implementation with hash computation for evidence.

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - render request formation
  - download bounds + early abort
  - host pinning and redirect refusal
  - content-type allowlist

## Acceptance criteria
- Asset export is safe-by-default and does not allow arbitrary egress.

Operation: figma.export_images supports PNG, SVG, JPG, PDF formats with configurable scale, SVG options, and bounds. 83 tests pass.","created_at":"2026-03-03T00:01:08Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-imi","title":"fcp.browser - Headless Browser Automation Connector","description":"# fcp.browser - Headless Browser Automation Connector (FCP2)

## Strategic Context

### Value Assessment
**Strategic Value Score: 95/100** — Critical Tier 1 meta-connector.

**Why Critical**: This is the **"UNIVERSAL ADAPTER"** that lets agents interact with ANY web service, even those without APIs. Combined with vision models, this enables true general-purpose web automation. When there is no API, the browser connector is the fallback.

### Priority Matrix Position
- **Category**: Strategic Investment (High Value + Foundation)
- **Build Phase**: Phase 1 Foundation (immediate)

---

## V2 Specification Compliance (NORMATIVE)

### ConnectorArchetype
```rust
archetypes: vec\![ConnectorArchetype::Operational]
```
- **Operational**: Navigates, extracts, interacts with web pages

### ConnectorStateModel
```rust
state_model: ConnectorStateModel::SingletonWriter
```
- **SingletonWriter**: Browser session state, cookies, auth tokens
- State tracked: `{ session_id: String, cookies: CookieJar, auth_state: Option<AuthState> }`
- Lease required for session fencing

### ConnectorFormat
```rust
format: ConnectorFormat::Native
```
- **Native MANDATORY**: Requires OS-level process spawning for browser
- Sandboxed via seccomp/landlock
- Browser runs in separate process sandbox
- Note: WASI does not support browser automation

### NetworkConstraints (NORMATIVE)
```rust
NetworkConstraints {
    // DYNAMIC: host_allow populated from policy allowlist
    host_allow: vec\![/* policy-defined allowlist */],
    port_allow: vec\![80, 443],
    deny_localhost: true,
    deny_private_ranges: true,
    deny_tailnet_ranges: true,
    deny_ip_literals: true,
    require_sni: true,
    require_host_canonicalization: true,
}
```
**Note**: Browser navigation is ONLY permitted to explicitly allowlisted hosts.

### TaintFlags (NORMATIVE)
```rust
// ALL web content is adversarial
page_content.provenance.taint = TaintFlags::PUBLIC_INPUT
    | TaintFlags::EXTERNAL_INPUT
    | TaintFlags::PROMPT_SURFACE
    | TaintFlags::UNVERIFIED_LINK;

// Form inputs submitted by agent
form_data.provenance.taint = TaintFlags::USER_SUPPLIED;

// Extracted data inherits source taint
extracted_data.provenance.taint = page_content.provenance.taint;
```

### SafetyTier per Operation
| Operation | SafetyTier | Rationale |
|-----------|------------|-----------|
| `browser.navigate` | Risky | Visits external sites |
| `browser.screenshot` | Safe | Read-only capture |
| `browser.extract` | Safe | Read-only extraction |
| `browser.click` | Risky | Triggers actions |
| `browser.fill` | Risky | Submits data |
| `browser.submit` | Dangerous | Form submission |
| `browser.authenticate` | Dangerous | Credential handling |
| `browser.execute_js` | Dangerous | Arbitrary code execution |

---

## Agent Capabilities (Concrete Use Cases)

### Universal Web Access
- Navigate any allowlisted website
- Fill forms and submit data
- Extract structured data (scraping)
- Handle authentication flows
- Screenshot and visual verification
- PDF generation from web pages

### Automation Superpowers
- **Covers EVERY service without a dedicated connector**
- Handles legacy systems with only web UI
- Enables agents to do anything a human can do online
- Future-proof: works with any new website automatically

### Examples of Agent Workflows
```
"Log into this legacy system and export the report"
"Fill out this government form with the provided data"
"Screenshot the competitor pricing page daily"
"Extract product data from this e-commerce site"
"Complete this multi-step web workflow"
"Check if this website is accessible"
"Generate PDF invoice from this web portal"
```

---

## Flywheel Ecosystem Synergies

### ALL Other Connectors
**Universal fallback:**
- When no API exists, use browser
- Legacy system integration
- Complex web workflows

### Google AI/Vision Integration
- Screenshot → vision model analysis
- Visual understanding of pages
- Layout comprehension

### Vector DB Integration
- Scraped content → embeddings
- Web page indexing
- Content similarity search

### Agent Mail Integration
- Web form results → agent threads
- Screenshot reports
- Workflow status notifications

---

## Technical Implementation Notes

### Architecture
```
FCP Connector
      ↓
Browser Controller
      ↓
Chromium/Playwright
      ↓
Web (allowlisted hosts only)
```

### Recommended Crates
- `chromiumoxide` — Chrome DevTools Protocol
- `fantoccini` — WebDriver client
- `headless_chrome` — Chrome automation
- `image` — Screenshot handling

### Browser Options
| Engine | Recommendation |
|--------|----------------|
| Chromium | **Preferred** |
| Firefox | Supported |
| WebKit | Limited support |

### Security Hardening
- Browser runs with minimal permissions
- No extension installation
- Disk access disabled
- Network restricted to allowlist

---

## Zone Architecture

### Recommended Zones
| Zone | Description | Capabilities |
|------|-------------|--------------|
| `z:web:browser:allowlist` | Allowlisted sites | Navigate + extract |
| `z:web:browser:auth` | Auth-enabled | Can handle login |
| `z:web:browser:restricted` | Minimal | Screenshot only |

### Security Considerations
- EVERY navigated URL is adversarial
- Content extraction requires sanitization
- Form submission is high-risk
- JavaScript execution is DANGEROUS

---

## Manifest Archetypes (Closed Set)
- `operational` — Web interactions

---

## External Surface

### NetworkConstraints
Default-deny; DYNAMIC allowlist:
- Policy specifies permitted hosts
- No implicit wildcards
- Subdomain matching explicit

### Credential Injection
- Site credentials via egress proxy
- Session cookies managed securely
- Never persist login state

---

## Capability Model

### Navigation Operations
- `browser.navigate` — Load URL (allowlisted)
- `browser.back` / `browser.forward` — History navigation
- `browser.refresh` — Reload page

### Extraction Operations
- `browser.screenshot` — Capture page
- `browser.extract` — DOM extraction
- `browser.pdf` — Generate PDF

### Interaction Operations
- `browser.click` — Click element
- `browser.fill` — Fill form field
- `browser.submit` — Submit form (Dangerous)
- `browser.authenticate` — Login flow (Dangerous)

### Advanced Operations
- `browser.execute_js` — Run JavaScript (Dangerous)
- `browser.wait` — Wait for condition
- `browser.scroll` — Scroll page

---

## Operations (Representative)

### Navigate to URL
```json
{
  "operation": "browser.navigate",
  "url": "https://allowlisted-site.com/page",
  "wait_for": "networkidle"
}
```

### Take Screenshot
```json
{
  "operation": "browser.screenshot",
  "selector": "#main-content",
  "format": "png",
  "full_page": false
}
```

### Extract Data
```json
{
  "operation": "browser.extract",
  "selectors": {
    "title": "h1.title",
    "price": ".price-value",
    "description": ".product-desc"
  }
}
```

### Fill Form
```json
{
  "operation": "browser.fill",
  "fields": [
    {"selector": "#email", "value": "test@example.com"},
    {"selector": "#message", "value": "Hello"}
  ]
}
```

---

## Tests

### Unit Tests (Mock-Only)
- Command serialization
- Response parsing
- Selector validation
- Allowlist enforcement

### Integration Tests
- Mock browser responses
- Multi-step navigation
- Form interaction simulation
- Error handling

### E2E Compliance
- Validates NetworkConstraints (allowlist only)
- Dangerous operation approval flow
- Content taint propagation
- Audit logging

---

## Acceptance Criteria
- Passes connector compliance runner (static + dynamic)
- No real web navigation in unit tests
- URL allowlist enforced
- Screenshot capture functional
- DOM extraction works
- Form filling works
- JavaScript execution requires approval
- All V2 NORMATIVE requirements  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-imi.1","title":"fcp.browser: Manifest + Capability Map (FCP2)","description":"# fcp.browser: Manifest + Capability Map (FCP2)

## Goal
Define the browser connector manifest contract:
- archetypes (`operational`, `bidirectional`)
- operations + schema IDs
- capability families + per-operation caps
- NetworkConstraints strategy (prefer explicit allowlists)
- resource requirements for Execution Planner
- state model for optional session persistence

## Acceptance Criteria
- Complete manifest spec stored here.
- Good/bad manifest examples exist for static compliance.
